name: Spicy Pineapple Over Coconut Almond-Crusted Ice Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the pineapple chips:
a. Preheat oven to 150°F (65°C).
b. Line two large baking sheets with parchment paper.
c. Remove top and skin from pineapple; slice into 1/8-inch thick pieces.
d. Sprinkle slices with granulated sugar and place on baking sheets.
e. Bake for 4-5 hours, until dry and crisp. Let cool completely.
2. Make the coconut-almond crust:
a. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
b. Combine shredded coconut and sliced almonds in a bowl.
c. Sprinkle mixture evenly on baking sheet and roast for 5-10 minutes.
d. Remove from oven; let cool, then store covered.
3. Make the lacy tuiles:
a. Cream butter and sugar until light (medium-high speed).
b. Add corn syrup, vanilla, and flour; mix medium-low speed.
c. Transfer to container and refrigerate 4 hours or overnight.
d. Form batter into balls, flatten slightly, and bake for 20 minutes.
e. Cool tuiles briefly, then lift and place over containers.
f. Allow tuiles to cool completely.
4. Make the spicy pineapple compote:
a. Remove top and skin from pineapple; discard core.
b. Cut into bite-size chunks.
c. In saucepan, combine butter, sugar, honey, and lemon juice.
d. Boil until color darkens (3 minutes); add pepper and pineapple.
e. Cook 3-4 minutes until warmed through.
5. Assemble desserts:
a. Place tuile cup on plate.
b. Sprinkle coconut-almond mixture into small bowl.
c. Press two scoops of ice cream onto the mixture to cover completely.
d. Place ice cream in tuile cup and pour 1/3 cup of compote over it.
e. Garnish with pineapple chips. Repeat for remaining desserts.
6. Serve immediately.
